title: Erianthus rufipilus
taxonomy: Erianthus rufipilus is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified as a type of bamboo grass due to its unique characteristics. The genus Erianthus is closely related to the genus Saccharum, which includes sugarcane. Erianthus rufipilus is native to Asia and is often found growing in mountainous regions. This species is further classified as a member of the subfamily Bambusoideae within the Poaceae family.
